From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Ricardo Jérez Jr.](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_Jr.&redirect=no "Ricardo Jérez Jr."))

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Jérez* and the second or maternal family name is  *Figueroa*.

**Ricardo Antonio Jérez Figueroa** (born 4 February 1986) is a Guatemalan professional [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who plays as a [goalkeeper](/wiki/Goalkeeper_(football) "Goalkeeper (football)") for [USL League One](/wiki/USL_League_One "USL League One") club [Chattanooga Red Wolves](/wiki/Chattanooga_Red_Wolves_SC "Chattanooga Red Wolves SC").

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1986)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

Jerez is the son of the also former national team goalkeeper [Ricardo Jérez](/wiki/Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1956) "Ricardo Jérez (footballer, born 1956)") who played in the [1988 Olympics](/wiki/1988_Summer_Olympics "1988 Summer Olympics").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Jerez Jr. came through the youth ranks of local giants [CSD Comunicaciones](/wiki/CSD_Comunicaciones "CSD Comunicaciones") before joining [Cobán Imperial](/wiki/Cob%C3%A1n_Imperial "Cobán Imperial") and [C.S.D. Municipal](/wiki/C.S.D._Municipal "C.S.D. Municipal").

### Rentistas (2008–2009)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1986)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Rentistas (2008–2009)")]

On early August 2008, he was sent to [Uruguay](/wiki/Uruguay "Uruguay") to participate in some tests with [Peñarol](/wiki/Pe%C3%B1arol "Peñarol"), which he failed but he was able to stay in the country to play for the [Rentistas](/wiki/Rentistas "Rentistas") team which competed in the [Uruguayan Segunda División](/wiki/Uruguayan_Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n "Uruguayan Segunda División").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He made his debut on 13 September against local rivals [Cerrito](/wiki/Club_Sportivo_Cerrito "Club Sportivo Cerrito"), match which his team lost 2–1, but he was able to save a penalty shot by Richard Requelme and was named man of the match.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Later, in the third round of the tournament, he saved again a penalty now against [Boston River](/wiki/Boston_River "Boston River").

### Back to Guatemala (2009–2010)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1986)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Back to Guatemala (2009–2010)")]

He then returned to Guatemala in September 2009 to play for [Deportivo Jalapa](/wiki/Deportivo_Jalapa "Deportivo Jalapa")[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In the summer of 2010, he joined Argentinian [third division](/wiki/Primera_B_Metropolitana "Primera B Metropolitana") side [Club Comunicaciones](/wiki/Club_Comunicaciones "Club Comunicaciones").[[5]](#cite_note-5) He remained six months on the club and could not play due to problems with his documents.

Later, Jerez returned to Guatemala where he played for [USAC](/wiki/Universidad_SC "Universidad SC") in a campaign that saw them suffer relegation.

### Deportivo Marquense (2011–2012)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1986)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Deportivo Marquense (2011–2012)")]

He then was signed by [Deportivo Marquense](/wiki/Deportivo_Marquense "Deportivo Marquense") in mid-2011, where between the playoffs of the 2011 Apertura and the 2012 Clausura tournament has set a new [CONCACAF](/wiki/CONCACAF "CONCACAF") club record with 941 consecutive minutes without allowing a goal, a streak put to an end by [Eliseo Quintanilla](/wiki/Eliseo_Quintanilla "Eliseo Quintanilla") of [Municipal](/wiki/C.S.D._Municipal "C.S.D. Municipal").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Alianza Petrolera (2013–2022)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1986)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Alianza Petrolera (2013–2022)")]

Jerez joined [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera "Alianza Petrolera") in January 2013, at the time being the only non-domestic player in the squad. He recorded a [clean sheet](/wiki/Clean_sheet "Clean sheet") in his first league match in Colombia, in a 1–0 win against [La Equidad](/wiki/La_Equidad "La Equidad") on 8 January 2013.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Throughout his time at the club, he has been regarded as an important figure in the team's success. In October 2013, the club announced the extension of Jerez's contract, which would end after the 2016 season. He's the first player to have played at least 100 games in the top division with the club.

### Chattanooga Red Wolves (2023–present)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1986)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Chattanooga Red Wolves (2023–present)")]

Jérez signed with [USL League One](/wiki/USL_League_One "USL League One") club [Chattanooga Red Wolves](/wiki/Chattanooga_Red_Wolves_SC "Chattanooga Red Wolves SC") on 10 January 2023.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1986)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: International career")]

Jerez made his debut for [Guatemala](/wiki/Guatemala_national_football_team "Guatemala national football team") in a January 2006 friendly match against the [United States](/wiki/United_States_men%27s_national_soccer_team "United States men's national soccer team"). He has represented his country in 2 [FIFA World Cup qualification](/wiki/FIFA_World_Cup_qualification "FIFA World Cup qualification") processes and at the [2009 UNCAF Nations Cup](/wiki/2009_UNCAF_Nations_Cup "2009 UNCAF Nations Cup").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

In 2008, he was selected for the [Guatemala U-23](/wiki/Guatemala_national_football_team "Guatemala national football team") squad to participate in the [2008 CONCACAF Men's Olympic Qualifying](/wiki/2008_CONCACAF_Men%27s_Olympic_Qualifying "2008 CONCACAF Men's Olympic Qualifying") where they could not qualify for the [2008 Summer Olympics](/wiki/Football_at_the_2008_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_tournament "Football at the 2008 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament").

He played in all of his team's matches at the [2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup](/wiki/2011_CONCACAF_Gold_Cup "2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup"), and was the starting goalkeeper for Guatemala in 11 of Guatemala's 12 matches during the [2014 FIFA World Cup qualification](/wiki/2014_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(CONCACAF) "2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1986)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Jérez's father, also named [Ricardo Jérez](/wiki/Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1956) "Ricardo Jérez (footballer, born 1956)"), was also a professional footballer.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_J%C3%A9rez_(footballer,_born_1986)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Honours")]

**Municipal**

* [Liga Nacional de Guatemala](/wiki/Liga_Nacional_de_F%C3%BAtbol_de_Guatemala "Liga Nacional de Fútbol de Guatemala"): Apertura 2006
